list.name: lender
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT lender_name FROM v_rate_observations ORDER BY 1

list.name: product
list.query: SELECT DISTINCT product_label FROM v_rate_observations ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Latest APR by loan type and lender
panels.description: APR, not the headline rate, since it folds in lender fees and is the more comparable figure across lenders. Most recent observed_date only. One bar group per loan type, one bar per lender.

targets.rawSql:
-- Pivoted in SQL rather than via a Grafana transform: partitionByValues splits
-- this into one frame per lender and the barchart panel doesn't rejoin them
-- by product_label, so only the first frame (alphabetically) ever rendered.
-- NOTE: add/remove a MAX(...) FILTER column here when a lender is enabled/disabled in lenders.yaml.
SELECT
  product_label,
  MAX(apr) FILTER (WHERE lender_name = 'America First Credit Union') AS "America First Credit Union",
  MAX(apr) FILTER (WHERE lender_name = 'Goldenwest Credit Union') AS "Goldenwest Credit Union",
  MAX(apr) FILTER (WHERE lender_name = 'Granite Credit Union') AS "Granite Credit Union",
  MAX(apr) FILTER (WHERE lender_name = 'Rocket Mortgage') AS "Rocket Mortgage",
  MAX(apr) FILTER (WHERE lender_name = 'Zions Bank') AS "Zions Bank"
FROM v_rate_observations
WHERE observed_date = (SELECT MAX(observed_date) FROM v_rate_observations)
  AND lender_name IN ($lender)
  AND product_label IN ($product)
GROUP BY product_label
ORDER BY product_label

SELECT observed_date AS "time", lender_name || ' - ' || product_label AS metric, interest_rate FROM v_rate_observations WHERE lender_name IN ($lender) AND product_label IN ($product) AND $__timeFilter(observed_date) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Latest rates by lender and product

targets.rawSql: SELECT lender_name, product_label, interest_rate, apr, observed_date FROM v_rate_observations WHERE observed_date = (SELECT MAX(observed_date) FROM v_rate_observations) AND lender_name IN ($lender) AND product_label IN ($product) ORDER BY lender_name, product_label

panels.title: Best rate by loan type (last 24h)
panels.description: The single lowest APR seen for each loan type in the last 24 hours, and which lender offered it -- a leaderboard, not a per-lender comparison (that's the panel above). "Common" loan types are scoped dynamically to ones actually quoted by 2+ lenders in the window, not a hardcoded list, so it doesn't go stale as lenders are added/disabled. Sorted best-first; one hue since the bars aren't a lender identity encoding (a rerun can hand the win to a different lender in the same slot).

targets.rawSql:
WITH last_24h AS (
  SELECT product_label, lender_name, apr
  FROM v_rate_observations
  WHERE fetched_at >= NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'
    AND apr IS NOT NULL
    AND lender_name IN ($lender)
    AND product_label IN ($product)
),
common_products AS (
  -- "common" = actually compared across 2+ lenders in the window, not a
  -- hardcoded list -- so it stays correct as lenders are added/disabled.
  SELECT product_label
  FROM last_24h
  GROUP BY product_label
  HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT lender_name) >= 2
),
ranked AS (
  SELECT
    product_label,
    lender_name,
    apr,
    ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY product_label ORDER BY apr ASC, lender_name ASC) AS rnk
  FROM last_24h
  WHERE product_label IN (SELECT product_label FROM common_products)
)
SELECT
  product_label || ' — ' || lender_name AS "Best offer",
  apr AS "APR"
FROM ranked
WHERE rnk = 1
ORDER BY apr ASC
